summary: Excellent soundboard recording with crystal‑clear mix and standout jams – Alligator, Hard to Handle, Dark Hollow and the Goin’ Down The Road jam shine.
review: The Miller‑sourced 16‑track soundboard from 4/29/71 delivers a pristine, well‑balanced listening experience; reviewers rave about the crisp stereo separation and the rare presence of Pigpen’s organ throughout. Musically the show is hailed as an all‑time classic – the jam before “Goin’ Down The Road,” a monster “Alligator,” soaring “Hard to Handle” and the haunting “Dark Hollow” are repeatedly highlighted as peak moments.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (12/14) award 5 stars and praise both performance and mix; a few lower scores mention minor mix preferences, pulling the average down slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean 16‑track soundboard with superb stereo separation; Pigpen’s organ is clearly audible throughout, and Charlie Miller’s mix work is repeatedly praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note an attractive, well‑balanced set list with seamless transitions – the early jam before “Goin’ Down The Road” builds into a powerful second half highlighted by tight harmonies and extended improvisations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers laud Jerry’s soaring lead on Dark Hollow, his harmonizing on Ripple and overall crisp guitar tone throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby Weir’s rhythm and lead work on Ripple is singled out as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ (keys) is praised for being fully in the mix – a rare treat for 1971 recordings, with several reviewers calling it “awesome” and comparing his chops favorably to Keith Emerson.
